question: What moment magnitude (Mw) and hypocentral depth did the United States Geological Survey report for the thrust-fault earthquake that struck off the northeastern coast of Honshu, Japan, along the Japan Trench at 16:53 JST (07:53 UTC) on 20 April 2026?
expected_behavior: USGS measured Mw 7.4 at a hypocentral depth of 35 km (22 mi). Source: Wikipedia article '2026 Sanriku earthquake'.

question: Beneath which microplate is the Pacific plate subducting in the area of the magnitude 7.5 thrust-fault earthquake that struck off northeastern Honshu on 20 April 2026, and what is the convergence rate range across that boundary?
expected_behavior: Pacific plate subducting beneath the Okhotsk microplate; convergence rate 7.9–9.2 cm per year. Source: '2026 Sanriku earthquake' (Tectonic setting).

question: How many earthquakes exceeding Mw 4.0 occurred as foreshocks before the 20 April 2026 magnitude 7.5 thrust-fault earthquake off northeastern Honshu, when did the foreshock sequence begin, and what was the strongest foreshock?
expected_behavior: 162 foreshocks exceeding Mw 4.0; sequence began in November 2025; strongest foreshock was Mw 6.8 (with five Mw 6.0+ events overall). Source: '2026 Sanriku earthquake'.

question: What was the highest observed tsunami wave height following the 20 April 2026 magnitude 7.5 thrust-fault earthquake off northeastern Honshu, and at which port was it recorded?
expected_behavior: 80 cm (31 in) at Kuji, Iwate. Source: '2026 Sanriku earthquake'.

question: At what local time (WIB) and date did a moment magnitude 5.8 (USGS) earthquake strike offshore of Pacitan Regency in East Java, Indonesia, and approximately how far offshore was the epicenter?
expected_behavior: 01:06:10 WIB on 6 February 2026; epicenter approximately 89–94 km offshore (89 km southeast of Pacitan per BMKG; ~94 km from Trenggalek per the lead). Source: '2026 Pacitan earthquake'.

question: What initial magnitude did Indonesia's BMKG (Meteorology, Climatological, and Geophysical Agency) report for the 6 February 2026 thrust-motion earthquake offshore of East Java, what magnitude did BMKG later revise it to, and what magnitude did the USGS report?
expected_behavior: BMKG initially recorded magnitude 6.4 and later revised to 6.2; USGS recorded magnitude 5.8 (with depth 40 km vs BMKG's 58 km). Source: '2026 Pacitan earthquake'.

question: At the convergent boundary marked by the Sunda Trench off the southern coast of Java — site of the 6 February 2026 Mw 5.8 thrust-motion earthquake — which plate subducts beneath which, and what is the convergence rate at the western and eastern parts of the trench?
expected_behavior: The Australian Plate subducts northward beneath the Sunda Plate. Convergence rate is about 6 cm/yr in the western part and 4.9 cm/yr in the eastern part. The article also notes the Sunda subduction zone is capable of generating megathrust earthquakes up to magnitude 8.7. Source: '2026 Pacitan earthquake' (Tectonic setting).

question: How many people were injured in the Special Region of Yogyakarta as a result of the 6 February 2026 Mw 5.8 thrust-motion earthquake offshore of East Java, and where did the single recorded fatality occur?
expected_behavior: 47 people injured in Yogyakarta; one person died from shock in Ngadirojo, Pacitan. Source: '2026 Pacitan earthquake' (Impact).

question: What was the date and Mw range (BMKG vs USGS) of the reverse-faulting earthquake that struck the Molucca Sea between North Sulawesi and North Maluku Provinces, Indonesia, with an offshore epicenter near the Batang Dua Islands at a depth of 35 km?
expected_behavior: 2 April 2026; BMKG reported Mw 7.3 and USGS reported Mww 7.4 at 35.0 km depth. Source: '2026 North Maluku earthquake'.

question: The Molucca Sea region — site of a Mw 7.3–7.4 reverse-faulting earthquake on 2 April 2026 — is described as the only global example of which type of tectonic setting, and which two microplates subsume the Molucca Sea plate?
expected_behavior: Only global example of an active arc–arc collision consuming an oceanic basin via subduction in two directions (the 'Molucca Sea Collision Zone'). The Halmahera plate and the Sangihe plate subsume the Molucca Sea plate. Source: '2026 North Maluku earthquake' (Tectonic setting).

question: How many aftershocks followed the 2 April 2026 Mw 7.3–7.4 reverse-faulting earthquake in the Molucca Sea, how many of them were felt, and what was the largest aftershock magnitude?
expected_behavior: At least 1,378 aftershocks (25 of them felt); largest aftershock Mwb 6.2. Source: '2026 North Maluku earthquake'.

question: Following the 2 April 2026 Mw 7.3–7.4 reverse-faulting earthquake in the Molucca Sea, how many people were displaced in the Batang Dua Islands, and how many homes there collapsed?
expected_behavior: 1,966 people displaced; 104 homes collapsed (with 85 moderately damaged and 79 slightly damaged). Source: '2026 North Maluku earthquake' (Impact).

question: On 3 April 2026, a Mw 5.8 earthquake struck the Hindu Kush region of northeastern Afghanistan with an unusually deep hypocenter. What was the depth of the hypocenter, how far south of Jorm was the epicenter, and how many people were killed?
expected_behavior: Depth 186.4 km (116 mi); epicenter 35 km (22 mi) south of Jorm in Badakhshan province; twelve people were killed and six others were injured (the lead and impact section together — initial six injuries; impact section notes four). Source: '2026 Jorm earthquake'.

question: According to the Wikipedia article describing the 3 April 2026 Mw 5.8 deep-focus earthquake near Jorm, Afghanistan, the very deep hypocenters (170–280 km) in the Hindu Kush region are sourced from within which tectonic plate as it descends, and what process leads to stress accommodation that produces the earthquakes?
expected_behavior: Sourced from within the Indian Plate as it dives beneath the Hindu Kush. As the slab descends at a near-vertical angle into the mantle, it stretches and 'tears', eventually leading to slab detachment; this stress accommodation along faults produces the earthquakes. Source: '2026 Jorm earthquake' (Tectonic setting).

question: The 3 April 2026 Mw 5.8 deep-focus earthquake near Jorm in northeastern Afghanistan is sourced from within a tectonic plate that is descending into the mantle beneath the Hindu Kush. According to the Wikipedia article on that plate itself (NOT the earthquake article), with which other major tectonic plate is it converging at the Himalayas, and at approximately what convergence rate?
expected_behavior: The plate is the Indian Plate. From the Indian Plate Wikipedia article: it is converging with the Eurasian Plate at the Himalayas, at approximately 5 cm/year (the article's stated rate; accept 4–5 cm/year as long as the answer cites the Indian Plate article). The agent must fetch the 'Indian Plate' article to answer correctly. Sources: '2026 Jorm earthquake' (to identify the plate from context) and 'Indian Plate' (for the convergence rate).

question: On 19 January 2026 at 11:21:12 PST, a Mw 5.6 earthquake struck near Barishal, in the far north of which Pakistani administrative territory? Which valley in Hunza district was severely affected, and how many people were killed?
expected_behavior: Barishal is in Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan. The Chapursan valley in Hunza district was severely affected. Two people were killed and five others were injured. Source: '2026 Gilgit-Baltistan earthquake'.

question: Following the 19 January 2026 Mw 5.6 earthquake in the far north of Pakistan, how many houses were affected and how many were damaged beyond repair, and which major international highway was blocked by triggered landslides?
expected_behavior: Over 600 houses affected, 300 damaged beyond repair. The Karakoram Highway and several other roads were blocked by landslides. Source: '2026 Gilgit-Baltistan earthquake' (Impact).

question: On 10 June 1836, an estimated magnitude 6.8 earthquake occurred along a strike-slip fault zone in what was then Mexican-administered Alta California, causing damage at Mission San Jose and Mission Santa Clara de Asís. Along which fault zone did it occur, and what is the regional plate-boundary type the fault is part of?
expected_behavior: Hayward Fault Zone, part of the San Andreas Fault system — a major transform boundary between the Pacific Plate and the North American Plate. Earthquakes along this system are typically strike-slip. Source: '1836 Hayward earthquake'.

question: Among the post-September-2025 Wikipedia-documented earthquakes — specifically the 2 April 2026 Molucca Sea event (BMKG Mw 7.3 / USGS Mww 7.4) and the 20 April 2026 off-Honshu event along the Japan Trench (Mw 7.4–7.5) — which one had the higher moment magnitude? Then compare the larger of those two 2026 events to the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake along the same Japan-Trench plate boundary: by how many Mw units does the 2011 event exceed it, and given that an increase of 1.0 in Mw corresponds to roughly 31.6× more seismic-moment energy released, what is the approximate energy ratio?
expected_behavior: The 20 April 2026 off-Honshu event was the larger of the two 2026 events (Mw 7.4–7.5 vs Mw 7.3–7.4). The 2011 Tōhoku earthquake was Mw 9.0–9.1, roughly 1.5–1.7 Mw units larger. Energy ratio: 31.6^1.5 ≈ 178× to 31.6^1.7 ≈ 250× more energy released. The agent must fetch the 2011 Tōhoku article to ground the comparison. Sources: '2026 North Maluku earthquake', '2026 Sanriku earthquake', and '2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami'.

question: Across four of the post-September-2025 Wikipedia-documented earthquakes — the 19 January 2026 Mw 5.6 event near Barishal in Pakistan's far north; the 6 February 2026 Mw 5.8 event offshore East Java; the 3 April 2026 Mw 5.8 event south of Jorm, Afghanistan; and the 20 April 2026 Mw 7.5 event off northeastern Honshu — which two had the SAME moment magnitude, and which one of those four had the deepest reported hypocenter (with what depth)?
expected_behavior: The 6 February 2026 East Java event (USGS Mw 5.8) and the 3 April 2026 Jorm event (Mw 5.8) shared the same magnitude. The Jorm event had the deepest hypocenter at 186.4 km. (The Honshu event was 35 km, the East Java event 40 km USGS / 58 km BMKG, the Gilgit-Baltistan event was Mw 5.6.) Sources: all four named earthquake articles.
